subject: Oracle Corporation
value_text: Oracle Corporation described as the '3 billion dollar software giant' at time of the study

subject: Oracle Corporation
value_text: Aberdeen states that Oracle is aggressively changing its business model and products to attack the Internet opportunity faster than companies half its size

subject: Oracle InterOffice
value_text: Oracle InterOffice was originally code-named Pegasus prior to its December 13, 1995 announcement

subject: Oracle InterOffice
value_text: Modular cross-platform components for messaging, document management, workflow, and calendaring

subject: Oracle InterOffice
value_text: Oracle InterOffice deconstructs earlier monolithic Oracle products: Document Management, Oracle Office, ConText, and Workflow

subject: Electronic Economic Community
value_text: Aberdeen defines the 'Electronic Economic Community' as a new economic paradigm created by the Internet enabling businesses, partners, suppliers, and customers to communicate and transact without traditional electronic walls or geographic borders

subject: Oracle InterOffice
value_text: Aberdeen believes Oracle has a 6–12 month lead over its nearest competitors with Oracle InterOffice

subject: Oracle InterOffice
value_text: The unique capability to easily incorporate business partners and customers into any Oracle InterOffice enabled business process sets it apart from any other product Aberdeen has researched

subject: Oracle InterOffice
value_text: Oracle InterOffice is described as the first groupware product designed to meet the challenges imposed by the Internet, with Internet integration built in rather than added as a gateway

subject: Oracle InterOffice
value_text: Oracle InterOffice supports over 25 different server platforms including Windows NT and most RISC-based Unix systems including HP-9000, Sun, IBM RS/6000, and many others

subject: Oracle InterOffice
value_text: Oracle InterOffice clients available for Windows NT, Windows 95, Windows 3.1, Motif, Macintosh, ASCII terminals, and Web browsers; with varying levels of functionality per device

subject: Oracle InterOffice
value_text: Oracle InterOffice makes extensive use of threads enabling multiprocessor configurations where each thread can be dispatched to its own processor

subject: Oracle InterOffice
value_text: Oracle InterOffice messaging infrastructure supports thousands of users on a single server, data storage limited only by platform physical constraints, and networks of hundreds of mail servers

subject: Oracle InterOffice
value_text: Oracle InterOffice supports MAPI 1.0 and the Service Provider Interface (SPI), enabling any MS Mail client, Windows 95 In-Box, and MAPI-compliant applications to access Oracle InterOffice

subject: Oracle InterOffice
value_text: Oracle was working with several manufacturers to produce PDA clients that would enable these devices to access Oracle InterOffice

subject: Oracle InterOffice
value_text: Oracle InterOffice comes pre-packaged so that all popular clients, including any World Wide Web browser, can access Oracle InterOffice functionality

subject: Oracle InterOffice
value_text: Oracle InterOffice implements a Common Access Layer—a standard interface enabling all applications to utilize every InterOffice feature via OLE Automation Interface and OCX components

subject: Oracle InterOffice
value_text: Any desktop or server application can access all data stored in Oracle InterOffice using standard SQL through the Oracle System 7 Database; applications can also use PL/SQL for direct server-side data manipulation

subject: Oracle InterOffice
value_text: Oracle InterOffice visual components are packaged as OCX controls for rapid application development in C++, Visual Basic, and other OCX-aware environments including Oracle PowerObjects

subject: Oracle InterOffice
value_text: Oracle InterOffice provides mapping for ODMA (Open Document Management Access) standard interface enabling integration with document management-aware applications

subject: Oracle InterOffice
value_text: Oracle InterOffice will eventually support the Work Flow API (WFAPI) standard for workflow interoperability

subject: Oracle InterOffice
value_text: Oracle intends to integrate Oracle InterOffice with Java (Sun's Distributed Object environment), enabling any Java object on any Java platform direct access to Oracle InterOffice functionality

subject: Oracle InterOffice
value_text: Oracle InterOffice is implemented as functional modules (mail, document management, calendars, schedulers, workflow) sharing a common database engine and common access layer; modules are independent yet functionally enhance each other

subject: Oracle InterOffice
value_text: Messaging features include mail & directory with synchronization, attachments, templates, conferencing, shared & public folders, filters, mobile support

subject: Oracle InterOffice
value_text: Scheduling features include day & week views, alarms, to-do lists, automatic meetings, attendee-free-time views, access control, time zones

subject: Oracle InterOffice
value_text: Document management features include support for any document type, check-in & out, version control, full-text search, access control, renewable document attributes

subject: Oracle InterOffice
value_text: Workflow features include graphical design, routing, access control, PL/SQL engine

subject: Groupware market
value_text: Every messaging and groupware vendor in the market is scrambling to integrate their offerings to the Internet; most have initially tried to implement SMTP and Web gateways offering very limited services

subject: SMTP/Web gateways
value_text: SMTP and Web gateway approaches offer very limited Internet services, require substantial system resources, and demand new administrative processes

subject: Electronic Economic Community
value_text: The three key roadblocks preventing IS from incorporating business partners and customers are: (1) Network Connectivity, (2) Authentication/Security, and (3) Application Access

subject: Internet Security
value_text: Aberdeen argues there is a trade-press misconception that Internet security is inadequate; Aberdeen believes Internet security can be established to a high degree and that IS organizations use security as an excuse to avoid Internet adoption

subject: IS organizations
value_text: Aberdeen believes many IS organizations have avoided key Internet-related business issues, using security as an excuse

subject: World Wide Web
value_text: The World Wide Web solves the partner/customer application access problem by establishing a single common browser environment and user interface for every platform deployed

subject: Web Browsers
value_text: Today's World Wide Web browsers are already evolving to include distributed objects including OLE and Java, enabling tighter data integration beyond HTML-only interactions

subject: Data Hostage Problem
value_text: Before the Web, data was held hostage by the applications that created it; IS had to either install the application on every user's desktop or build a costly data gateway between applications
source_section: Incorporating Partners and Customers into IS Solutions

subject: World Wide Web
value_text: The Web delivers a whole new environment to IS allowing applications to keep control of data while simultaneously allowing access by any user with a standard Web browser, using a common data format and interaction metaphor
source_section: Incorporating Partners and Customers into IS Solutions

subject: IS organizations
value_text: Once Web infrastructure is installed, IS resources become free to help department managers integrate corporate data into business processes and extend them to business partners and customers
source_section: Incorporating Partners and Customers into IS Solutions

subject: Internet
value_text: Aberdeen predicts that when traditional electronic walls are finally exterminated, businesses and consumers will communicate in ways not yet predicted, tied together by technology into an Electronic Economic Community

subject: Oracle InterOffice
value_text: Oracle InterOffice approaches data integration in a new way: server applications access via standard SQL, desktop clients through Web browsers, OLE Automation and OCX controls today, and Java objects tomorrow

subject: Oracle InterOffice
value_text: Oracle InterOffice's multi-interface approach (SQL, OLE, OCX, Web, Java) defines a new breed of product that every other vendor will need to catch up to

subject: IS managers
value_text: Aberdeen recommends that companies deploying Oracle InterOffice for Internet-enabled business communications can achieve a similar 6–12 month lead over their own competitors

subject: Oracle InterOffice
value_text: Oracle InterOffice can be administered from a central location or in a distributed fashion using Oracle Office management and administration tools

subject: Oracle InterOffice
value_text: Oracle InterOffice functions can be encapsulated in applications built by IS (custom) or deployed using the Oracle InterOffice client software (packaged)

subject: Oracle InterOffice
value_text: Oracle InterOffice client software available for almost any desktop, PDA, consumer screen phones, or other environment in use in 1995

subject: Oracle InterOffice
value_text: Aberdeen is impressed with Oracle's ability to construct a robust, open, and flexible product with the InterOffice architecture

subject: Oracle InterOffice
value_text: InterOffice uses the MAPI Service Provider Interface to 'publish' unique Oracle InterOffice functionality to MAPI-aware devices; MAPI applications can 'subscribe' to these services

subject: Oracle InterOffice
value_text: Oracle InterOffice was announced on December 13, 1995 — the same date as the Aberdeen study publication

subject: Oracle InterOffice
value_text: A properly designed Internet Firewall coupled with a properly designed application like Oracle InterOffice offers safe and secure communications for most business transactions
